title: dianthus × gabrielae
intro: Dianthus × gabrielae, also known as the Gabriel's Dianthus, is a hybrid plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) and is derived from the cross between Dianthus barbatus and Dianthus plumarius. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Dianthus × gabrielae include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiviral properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Dianthus × gabrielae is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and its leaves can also be used in salads or as a garnish, adding a sweet and slightly spicy flavor.
